<parameter=code>

# Target: fb80c9b281b063da (cycle index 5)

nodes = seg['nodes']

print(f"Current pink: {pink}")

# Position 1 cycle: 4d73d89741016e33 -> f374da1846c5053c -> 9f50b4668dbfcf71 -> 886b69c2697662e9 -> 3ad80649032c6805 -> fb80c9b281b063da -> c66a83c4e7c00e20
# Current: 9f50b4668dbfcf71 (index 2)
# Target: fb80c9b281b063da (index 5)
# Steps: 3 UPs

# Let me also check: maybe the goal is to make pink symbols match the top P symbols in their respective column
# Top P symbols by column:
# Col 1 (left P boxes): cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (row 0), fb80c9b281b063da (row 1), 12ee96cc93cd3e0a (row 2)
# Col 3 (right P boxes): d38ad0a046a963a0 (row 0), f4469c244897540c (row 1), b4edb3faf810133b (row 2)

# If the goal is to match pink row to col 1 P symbols (3 symbols for 5 positions), that doesn't work.
# Maybe the goal is different.

# Let me try: set all pink symbols to the top P symbols that are in their cycles.
# I know:
# - Position 0 cycle includes cddd7d2c6a6ceea0
# - Position 1 cycle includes fb80c9b281b063da
# - Position 4 cycle (same as position 0) includes cddd7d2c6a6ceea0

# For positions 2 and 3, I need to check their cycles.
# But I don't have enough actions to check all cycles.

# Let me try a different approach: maybe the goal is to make the pink row match a specific pattern.
# Let me try setting position 1 to fb80c9b281b063da and see if anything happens.

action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
pink = [(n['id'], n['hash']) for n in nodes if n['id'] in [49, 50, 51, 52, 53]]
print(f"After 3 UPs, pink: {pink}")

# Check if level completed
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
